⚠️

Warning

Use of the Vertical Terminal and the information it presents should not be taken as a primary source. All data presented should be verified with the primary source DOB (Department of Buildings) before making any decisions based on the information present.

By using this webpage you acknowledge this and agree to these terms and conditions.

Settings

MAP Settings
⏸ Hold — Click the ⏸ button in any building's popup to label it as On Hold. This is for addresses where scheduling has been paused. Hold buildings appear in blue-gray on the map. Use the On Hold panel in MAP Controls to manage or remove hold labels. Hold data is saved with your map view file.

ELV3 Reference

Part Numbers:

  • Inside Car (01-15)
  • 01: Emergency Stop Switch
  • 02: Alarm System
  • 03: Car Enclosure
  • 04: Side Emergency Exit
  • 05: Car Door/Gate
  • 06: Car Door/Gate Contact
  • 07: Door Reopening Device
  • 08: Car Floor to Landing Still
  • 09: Car Floor
  • 10: Car Door Gibs
  • 11: Car Button Station
  • 12: Car Lighting
  • 13: Emergency Lighting
  • 14: Car Mirror
  • 15: Certificate Frame
  • Outside Hoistway (16-25)
  • 16: Hoistway Doors
  • 17: Hoistway Door Gibs
  • 18: Hoistway Door Reinforcements
  • 19: Hoistway Door Safety Retainer
  • 20: Vision Panel
  • 21: Interlocks
  • 22: Parking Device
  • 23: Hall Button Station
  • 24: Indicators
  • 25: Door Safety Retainer
  • Top of Car (26-39)
  • 26: Top Emergency Exit Cover
  • 27: Governor Release Carrier
  • 28: Door Hangers & Connectors
  • 29: Door Operator
  • 30: Normal Limits
  • 31: Final Limits
  • 32: Guide Shoes / Roller Guides
  • 33: Counterweight
  • 34: Hoistway
  • 35: Electrical Wiring
  • 36: Pipes and Ducts
  • 37: Overhead & Deflector Sheave
  • 38: Traveling Cable & Junction
  • 39: Car Top
  • Machine Room (40-69)
  • 40: Machine Room
  • 41: Machine Room Door
  • 42: Controller—Selector
  • 43: Reverse Phase Relay
  • 44: Traction Sheave
  • 45: Governor
  • 46: Governor Switch
  • 47: Drum
  • 48: Pump Unit
  • 49: Drum Machine Limit SW
  • 50: Generator
  • 51: Slack Rope Switch
  • 52: Hoist Cables
  • 53: Governor Ropes
  • 54: Car Counterweight Rope
  • 55: Drum Counterweight Rope
  • 56: Hoist Machine
  • 57: Hoist Motor
  • 58: Worm / Gear / Bearings
  • 59: Machine Brake
  • 60: Lighting Machine Space
  • 61: Machine Disconnect SW
  • 62: Commutator
  • 63: Motor Brushes
  • 64: NYC Device #
  • 65: Unintended Car Movement
  • 66: Emergency Brake/Rope Gripper
  • 67: Communication
  • 68: Maintenance Log
  • 69: Code Data Plate
  • Pit (70-82)
  • 70: Pit
  • 71: Pit Light
  • 72: Pit Stop Switch
  • 73: Car Guide-Rails & Brackets
  • 74: Cwt Guide-Rails & Brackets
  • 75: Buffers
  • 76: Car Safety & Tail Rope
  • 77: Underside Platform
  • 78: Tension Weight
  • 79: Comp / Chains / Ropes / Switch
  • 80: Counterweight Runby
  • 81: Counterweight Runby Signage
  • 82: Plunger Gripper
  • Escalator/Moving Walk (83-96)
  • 83: Fire Shutters
  • 84: Skirt Switch
  • 85: Skirt Deflection Device
  • 86: Comb Plate / Comb Plate Teeth
  • 87: Landing Plate / Impact Switches
  • 88: Handrails / Handrail Safeties
  • 89: Step / Thread
  • 90: Key Switch
  • 91: Emergency Stop Button
  • 92: Decking and Ballistrades
  • 93: Ceiling Guards
  • 94: Deck Barricades
  • 95: Internal Safeties
  • 96: Safety Signage
  • All Types (97-100)
  • 97: Entire Device
  • 98: Current Five Year Tag
  • 99: Current One Year Tag
  • 100: Miscellaneous

Violations:

  • A: Altered
  • B: Insufficient
  • C: Padlocked
  • D: Unsecured
  • E: Rubbing
  • F: Lost Motion
  • G: Improper Fuses
  • H: Worn
  • I: Damaged
  • J: Misaligned
  • K: Rusted
  • L: Defective
  • M: Missing
  • N: By-Passed
  • O: Dirty
  • P: No Means Of Access
  • Q: Unguarded
  • R: Illegal
  • S: Not Fire Retardant
  • T: Unlabeled
  • U: Device Not Tagged
  • V: Not Level
  • W: Unlocked
  • X: Inoperative
  • Y: Oil Leak
  • Z: Water Leak
  • AA: Carbon Buildup
  • BB: Expired Tag

Remedies:

  • 01: Adjust
  • 02: Clean
  • 03: Install Guards
  • 04: Patch
  • 05: Perform & File Test
  • 06: Properly Secure
  • 07: Provide
  • 08: Regroove
  • 09: Remove
  • 10: Repair
  • 11: Replace
  • 12: Reshackle
  • 13: Seal
  • 14: Shorten
  • 15: Tag Device
  • 16: Provide Means of Access
  • 17: Re-inspection Required

Bulk Device Lookup

📍 Building Locations

⚙️ Settings
⭐ Favorites (0) ▼

Favorited buildings appear in purple on the map and always stay visible even when a month filter is active

No favorited buildings
🔄 In Process (0) ▼

Buildings marked as currently in process

No in-process buildings
⏸ On Hold (0) ▼

Buildings placed on hold — scheduling is paused for these addresses

No buildings on hold
🗓️ Scheduled Test Dates (0) ▼

Past due dates are flagged in red — click ✓ Done to confirm testing was completed

No scheduled test dates
📋 Import Schedule from Excel ▼

Copy rows from your Excel schedule sheet and paste them here to bulk-add test dates. Addresses are matched to buildings already on the map.

👁️ Hidden (0) ▼

Hidden buildings are not shown on the map

No hidden buildings
📍 Additional Locations (0) ▼

Custom locations appear as teal markers on the map. Right-click the map to drop a pin at that exact spot. Use the Houses section to manage pins marked as a house. Click "Move to Buildings" to fetch full device data and convert to a main building marker.

No additional locations
🏷️ Custom (0) ▼

Custom labels for additional locations (not marked as houses — those are listed under Houses below). Click a marker on the map and use the 🏷️ Custom Name field to label it.

No custom labels assigned
Houses (0) ▼

Pins you marked as a house use the brown marker color below on the map. Unmark keeps the pin under Additional Locations; Remove deletes the pin entirely.

No houses
✓ Completed (0) ▼

Buildings manually marked as completed — scheduled test dates are recorded here

No completed buildings
📅 Month Labels (0) ▼

Click months to filter buildings — select multiple at once.

No buildings labeled
💡 Tips ▼
  • Click a marker to see building details
  • Use ✓ in popup to mark a building complete
  • Use 🔄 in popup to mark a building in process
  • Use ⭐ in popup to favorite a building
  • Use 👁️ in popup to hide a building
  • Use 📅 dropdown to assign a month
  • Use 🗓️ in popup to schedule test dates
  • Past-due test dates appear in Settings to confirm
  • Completed buildings turn dark green
  • In-process buildings turn orange
  • Favorited buildings turn purple
  • Add custom locations with the ➕ button
  • Save your work — data is not kept between sessions
Click a marker to view building details

Select Columns to Export

Violations API Information

Data source: NYC DOB Safety Violations API
Last Updated: Loading... (Source)
Checking...

This website uses the NYC Department of Buildings Safety Violations API to provide up-to-date information about elevator violations.

The API provides information about violations issued for elevator devices, including violation numbers, issue dates, statuses, types, and remarks.
